sections.paragraph:
adj. (from the root אָבַר).-

(1) strong, mighty, used of men commonly as a subst. as, a mighty one, Jud. 5:22 Lam 1:15 Jer 46:15 Psa 76:6, אַבִּירֵי לֵב “the strong of heart.” Poetically used, κατʼ ἐξοχὴν-(a) of a bull; Psa 22:13, אַבִּירֵי בָּשָׁן “the strong ones, i.e. bulls, of Bashan;” Psa 50:13 and metaph. of princes, Psa 68:31.-(b) of the horse, only in Jer 8:16 47:3 50:11 (comp. Gramm. § 104. 2, note).

(2) powerful, noble, Job 24:22 34:20 אַבִּירִים לֶחֶם “food of nobles, or princes,” i.e. more delicate, dainty food, Psa 78:25 comp. Jud. 5:25 אַבִּיר הָרֹעִים “chief of the herdsmen,” 1Sa 21:8.

(3) אַבִּיר לֵב obstinate, stubborn, a man of perverse mind, Isa 46:12 comp. חֲזַק לֵב.
